title: Getting started with buckethost
text: Functions resolve connection details options (BUCKETHOST_* environment variables). Set — ideally .Rprofile: Three identifiers, easily confused: endpoint — storage host, bucket name. container — bucket name. remote — local name rclone remote (rclone config). mutating functions (bucketUpload(), bucketDelete(), bucketVerify(), uploading indexes) use . bucketBaseUrl() bucketRcloneRemote() show combine:
code: library(buckethost)  options(   buckethost.endpoint  = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca",   buckethost.container = "predictiveecology",   buckethost.remote    = "arbutus"   # name of the rclone remote ) bucketBaseUrl()        #> "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca/predictiveecology" bucketRcloneRemote()   #> "arbutus:predictiveecology"

title: Getting started with buckethost
text: bucketLs() reads bucket’s public listing API (credentials needed public-read bucket) returns one row per object: Build URLs keys:
code: objs <- bucketLs(prefix = "SCANFI_v2/1985") head(objs) #>                                          key      size                  modified #> 1 SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif 154238911 2026-01-19T... bucketUrl("SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif") #> "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca/predictiveecology/SCANFI_v2/1985/..."

title: Getting started with buckethost
text: bucketRaster() wraps terra::rast("/vsicurl/<url>"). Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, GDAL fetches byte ranges needs:
code: r <- bucketRaster("SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif") r                       # metadata only; nothing downloaded yet  aoi <- terra::vect(my_study_area) sub <- terra::crop(r, terra::project(aoi, terra::crs(r)))   # a few hundred KB terra::plot(sub)

title: Getting started with buckethost
text: Uploads deletes wrap rclone fail loudly rclone’s error text. rclone transfers changes, re-running cheap.
code: bucketUpload("~/local/SCANFI/1985", "SCANFI_v2/1985",               filterFile = "~/scanfi.filter",               extra = c("--retries", "10", "--stats", "60s"))  res <- bucketVerify("~/local/SCANFI/1985", "SCANFI_v2/1985",                      filterFile = "~/scanfi.filter") res$ok  bucketDelete("SCANFI_v2/1985/obsolete.tif")          # prompts to confirm bucketDelete("SCANFI_v2/old", recursive = TRUE,               confirm = FALSE)                         # whole prefix, no prompt

title: Getting started with buckethost
text: generateIndexes() writes one index.html per folder level (sortable columns, breadcrumbs, per-page disclaimer block) uploads : Browse result <endpoint>/<container>/index.html. Inspect pages without uploading passing dryRun = TRUE, returns HTML: customise look, copy shipped template edit :
code:
generateIndexes( heading = "PredictiveEcology Temporary Data Repository", disclaimerHtml = paste0( "

These data are not produced by the ", "PredictiveEcology group and are only hosted here to ease ", "open data access.

" ), hostNote = "Hosted on the Digital Research Alliance of Canada's Arbutus object storage." ) pages <- generateIndexes(dryRun = TRUE) cat(pages[[""]]) # the root index.html file.copy( system.file("templates", "index.html", package = "buckethost"), "my-template.html" ) generateIndexes(template = "my-template.html")

what: Why object storage over Google Drive?
title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: Google Drive doesn’t support HTTP range requests reliably, partial reads terra::rast("/vsicurl/...") don’t work — every read pulls whole file. Arbutus S3/Swift-compatible, Canadian-hosted, free academic PIs (Rapid Access Service allocation). files Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs (COGs) Arbutus, terra::rast() + crop() transfers bytes needed area interest — typically hundred KB per query.

what: Why a fast transfer node, not your usual login host?
title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: government-network host gave ~1 MB/s sustained testing; 321 GB migration taken days. Alliance’s Fir cluster CANARIE research network ~600+ MB/s Drive Arbutus — 321 GB completes minutes. Run transfer .

title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: . CCDB account active. https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca: confirm PI status active allocations; Account → Access Systems enable cluster ’ll transfer (e.g. Fir); Multifactor Authentication Management enroll Duo device save 10 backup codes. B. Arbutus cloud project + storage. Request via RAS (Rapid Access Service) form CCDB. form requires non-zero VM resources even object-storage-use, choose minimum (1 vCPU, 1 instance, 1 volume, 1.5 GB RAM, 1 floating IP, 20 GB persistent). number matters Object Storage — request need. Justify: hosting COG outputs collaborator access via /vsicurl/; HPC clusters don’t fit (’re compute, public HTTPS serving); include maintenance plan. Turnaround typically week two. C. EC2 (S3-compatible) credentials. OpenStack Application Credentials dashboard want — use Swift-native auth, S3. Horizon dashboard removed direct “EC2 Credentials” button, use CLI: needs port 5000 (Keystone), blocked many government networks. Workaround: run home network cellular hotspot. resulting EC2 access key + secret portable — created work anywhere port 443. Save password manager. D. Public container. Arbutus dashboard: Project → Object Store → Containers → Create container named e.g. predictiveecology. Set access Public /vsicurl/ reads work without authentication.
code: pipx install python-openstackclient        # or pip install --user on Windows source ~/Downloads/<project>-openrc.sh openstack ec2 credentials create

what: Phase 1 — configure rclone on the transfer node
title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: Fir ships rclone (verify rclone version). need two remotes: arbutus (S3) gdrive (Google Drive source).

previous_headings: Phase 1 — configure rclone on the transfer node
title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: rclone config → n (new) → name arbutus → storage s3 → provider → env_auth false → paste EC2 access key / secret → region blank → endpoint https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca → location_constraint blank → acl private → Edit advanced config: yes, set one critical option: Arbutus’s Swift S3 gateway doesn’t support virtual-hosted-style URLs (bucket.endpoint.com). Without force_path_style = true, every request fails signature DNS errors. Accept defaults everything else. resulting ~/.config/rclone/rclone.conf contain: Test: rclone lsd arbutus: list container(s) error.
code: force_path_style = true [arbutus] type = s3 provider = Other access_key_id = <key> secret_access_key = <secret> endpoint = https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca acl = private force_path_style = true

previous_headings: Phase 1 — configure rclone on the transfer node
title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: Google Drive OAuth needs browser, node headless. Bridge callback SSH port forwarding laptop: session: rclone config → n → name gdrive → storage drive → client_id/secret blank → scope drive.readonly (safer one-way migration) → service_account_file blank → advanced config n → “Use web browser automatically authenticate?” y. rclone prints http://127.0.0.1:53682/auth?state=... URL — paste laptop browser; SSH tunnel forwards rclone waiting Fir. Authorize, “Configure Shared Drive?” n (unless ’s Team Drive) → confirm. Test: rclone lsd gdrive: lists Drive folders. data “Shared ”, either add Drive shortcut , add --drive-shared--commands targeting path.
code: ssh -L 53682:localhost:53682 <user>@fir.alliancecan.ca

title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: Work inside tmux transfer survives disconnects: tmux new -s migrate (detach Ctrl-b d, reattach tmux attach -t migrate). 2a. Filter file (explicit top--bottom ordering, first match wins): 2b. Run copy, looping subfolders. Note destination uses underscores (SCANFI_v2) instead source’s space cleaner URLs: 2c. Verify counts/sizes, size-check source: equivalent R, buckethost configured:
code: cat > ~/scanfi.filter << 'EOF' - *.tif.tiff + *.tif + *.tif.ovr + *.tif.aux.xml - * EOF for year in 1985 1990 1995 2000 2005 2010 2015 2020 2025; do   echo "=== $(date) Starting $year ==="   rclone copy \     "gdrive:Shared/Hosted/SCANFI/SCANFI v2/$year" \     "arbutus:predictiveecology/SCANFI_v2/$year" \     --filter-from ~/scanfi.filter \     --transfers 16 --timeout 5m --contimeout 60s \     --retries 10 --low-level-retries 20 \     --stats 60s --log-file ~/rclone_scanfi_$year.log --log-level INFO   echo "=== $(date) Completed $year ===" done for year in 1985 1990 1995 2000 2005 2010 2015 2020 2025; do   rclone size arbutus:predictiveecology/SCANFI_v2/$year done  rclone check \   "gdrive:Shared/Hosted/SCANFI/SCANFI v2/1985" \   "arbutus:predictiveecology/SCANFI_v2/1985" \   --filter-from ~/scanfi.filter --size-only bucketVerify("gdrive-or-local/1985", "SCANFI_v2/1985",               filterFile = "~/scanfi.filter")$ok

title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: R session arbutus rclone remote configured: Browse https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca/predictiveecology/index.html. Re-run generateIndexes() time data added changed.
code:
library(buckethost) options( buckethost.endpoint = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca", buckethost.container = "predictiveecology", buckethost.remote = "arbutus" ) generateIndexes( heading = "PredictiveEcology Temporary Data Repository", disclaimerHtml = paste0( "
", "

These data are not produced by the PredictiveEcology group", " and are only hosted here to ease open data access.

", "

For SCANFI v2 data, see the authoritative source: ", "", "open.canada.ca

" ), hostNote = "Hosted on the Digital Research Alliance of Canada's Arbutus object storage." )

what: Phase 4 — the actual payoff: read it from anywhere
title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
code: library(terra) r <- bucketRaster("SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif") r                                            # metadata only  b   <- SpaDES.tools::randomStudyArea(size = 1e9, seed = 123) sub <- crop(r, project(vect(b), crs(r)))     # only the AOI's bytes transfer plot(sub)

title: Migrating data to an object store (the Arbutus recipe)
text: Every S3 request fails signature/DNS errors → forgot force_path_style = true rclone S3 remote. openstack ec2 credentials create hangs/refuses → port 5000 blocked network. Run home hotspot; credentials portable afterwards. OAuth callback never completes headless node → SSH -L 53682:localhost:53682 tunnel isn’t , ran rclone config different session one tunnel. Application Credentials don’t work rclone → Swift-native; need EC2 credentials S3 protocol. Data “Shared ” rclone can’t see → add Drive shortcut, use --drive-shared--.

what: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: Host catalogue files S3-compatible object store. buckethost turns bucket browsable, remotely readable data repository. built host open geospatial data (Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFFs) Digital Research Alliance Canada’s Arbutus object storage, nothing Arbutus-specific — works S3-compatible store: Amazon S3, OpenStack Swift, Ceph, MinIO, Cloudflare R2, Backblaze B2. three things:

title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: Upload/delete/verify shell rclone; install configure remote rclone config (see migration vignette). Listing index generation need public-read bucket — credentials.
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("PredictiveEcology/buckethost")

title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: functions resolve connection details options (BUCKETHOST_* environment variables), set per session:
code: options(   buckethost.endpoint  = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca",   buckethost.container = "predictiveecology",   buckethost.remote    = "arbutus"   # the rclone remote name )

title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
code: library(buckethost)  r <- bucketRaster("SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif") r                          # opens metadata only — no full download  aoi <- terra::vect(my_study_area) sub <- terra::crop(r, terra::project(aoi, terra::crs(r)))   # fetches only the AOI bytes terra::plot(sub)

title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: writes one index.html per “folder” bucket (sortable columns, breadcrumbs, per-page disclaimer) uploads . Browse result <endpoint>/<container>/index.html. Re-run whenever data changes. HTML produced template can override (system.file("templates", "index.html", package = "buckethost")); pass template = "-template.html" customise . Tokens: {{pageTitle}}, {{repoHeading}}, {{disclaimerHtml}}, {{viewingHeading}}, {{breadcrumb}}, {{parentLink}}, {{rows}}, {{hostNote}}, {{timestamp}}.
code:
generateIndexes( heading = "PredictiveEcology Temporary Data Repository", disclaimerHtml = paste0( "

These data are not produced by the ", "PredictiveEcology group and are only hosted here to ease open ", "data access.

" ), hostNote = "Hosted on the Digital Research Alliance of Canada's Arbutus object storage." )

title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: See vignette("migration-workflow", package = "buckethost") full end--end recipe: requesting Arbutus allocation, EC2 credentials, configuring rclone S3 + Google Drive, running large transfer fast transfer node, verifying , generating catalogue.

what: Why object storage over Google Drive?
title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: Google Drive doesn’t serve HTTP range requests reliably, terra::rast("/vsicurl/...") ends pulling whole files. S3/Swift store serves byte ranges, reading COG transfers bytes area interest needs — typically hundred KB per query.

title: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage
text: GPL-3 © Eliot McIntire / PredictiveEcology

title: Connection configuration — bucketConfig
text: Helpers resolve bucket lives. reads, order, R option, environment variable, built-default. Set options (see buckethost-package) rest package picks .

title: Connection configuration — bucketConfig
code: bucketEndpoint(endpoint = NULL)  bucketContainer(container = NULL)  bucketRemote(remote = NULL)  bucketBaseUrl(container = NULL, endpoint = NULL)  bucketRcloneRemote(container = NULL, remote = NULL)

title: Connection configuration — bucketConfig
text: endpoint object store's HTTPS host (bucket name). NULL, resolved options(buckethost.endpoint=), BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, built-default. container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. remote name local rclone remote. NULL, resolved options(buckethost.remote=), BUCKETHOST_REMOTE, built-default.

title: Connection configuration — bucketConfig
text: length-one character string. bucketBaseUrl(): public endpoint/container URL. bucketRcloneRemote(): remote:container string rclone.

title: Connection configuration — bucketConfig
text: Two URL/identifier conventions play easy confuse: endpoint object store's HTTPS host, e.g. "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca". bucket name. container bucket (.k.. container) name, e.g. "predictiveecology". remote name rclone remote configured rclone config, e.g. "arbutus". local machine unrelated public URL. bucketBaseUrl() combines endpoint + container public base URL reads; bucketRcloneRemote() combines remote + container remote:container string rclone expects.

title: Connection configuration — bucketConfig
code: bucketBaseUrl(endpoint = "https://example.com", container = "mydata") #> [1] "https://example.com/mydata" bucketRcloneRemote(remote = "arbutus", container = "mydata") #> [1] "arbutus:mydata"

what: Delete an object or prefix from a bucket — bucketDelete
title: Delete an object or prefix from a bucket — bucketDelete
text: Removes single file (rclone deletefile) entire prefix (rclone delete, recursive = TRUE) bucket. destructive asks confirmation default; set confirm = FALSE delete non-interactively (e.g. scripts).

title: Delete an object or prefix from a bucket — bucketDelete
code: bucketDelete(   remotePath,   container = NULL,   remote = NULL,   recursive = FALSE,   confirm = TRUE,   rclonePath = "rclone",   dryRun = FALSE )

title: Delete an object or prefix from a bucket — bucketDelete
text: remotePath Key (single file) prefix (recursive = TRUE) delete, e.g. "SCANFI_v2/1985/old.tif". full public URL accepted normalised key. container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. remote name local rclone remote. NULL, resolved options(buckethost.remote=), BUCKETHOST_REMOTE, built-default. recursive TRUE, delete everything remotePath (prefix). FALSE (default), delete single object. confirm TRUE (default) session interactive, prompt deleting. rclonePath Path rclone executable. Default "rclone". dryRun TRUE, pass --dry-run. Default FALSE.

title: Delete an object or prefix from a bucket — bucketDelete
text: Invisibly, rclone's captured output, NULL cancelled.

what: List objects in a bucket — bucketLs
title: List objects in a bucket — bucketLs
text: Reads bucket's public S3 listing API returns one row per object. Pagination (1000 keys per response) handled . bucket must configured public/anonymous read.

title: List objects in a bucket — bucketLs
code: bucketLs(   prefix = "",   container = NULL,   endpoint = NULL,   includeIndexes = FALSE )

title: List objects in a bucket — bucketLs
text: prefix Optional key prefix restrict listing, e.g. "SCANFI_v2/1985". Default "" lists everything. container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. endpoint object store's HTTPS host (bucket name). NULL, resolved options(buckethost.endpoint=), BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, built-default. includeIndexes FALSE (default), generated index.html files dropped result see data objects.

title: List objects in a bucket — bucketLs
text: data.frame columns key, size (bytes), modified (ISO-8601 string), sorted key.

title: List objects in a bucket — bucketLs
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bucketLs(prefix = "SCANFI_v2/1985",           endpoint = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca",           container = "predictiveecology") } # }

what: Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/ — bucketRaster
title: Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/ — bucketRaster
text: Convenience wrapper around terra::rast("/vsicurl/<url>"). object Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF, GDAL fetches byte ranges needed — opening metadata cropping small area transfers hundred KB rather whole file.

title: Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/ — bucketRaster
code: bucketRaster(path, container = NULL, endpoint = NULL, ...)

title: Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/ — bucketRaster
text: path Object key relative bucket root, e.g. "SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif". container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. endpoint object store's HTTPS host (bucket name). NULL, resolved options(buckethost.endpoint=), BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, built-default. ... Passed terra::rast().

title: Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/ — bucketRaster
text: terra::SpatRaster.

title: Read a remote raster via GDAL /vsicurl/ — bucketRaster
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ r <- bucketRaster("SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif") r                                  # metadata only, no full download terra::crop(r, my_aoi)             # fetches just the AOI's bytes } # }

what: Upload files to a bucket — bucketUpload
title: Upload files to a bucket — bucketUpload
text: Thin, loud wrapper around rclone copy. Copies local (file directory) remotePath within bucket. rclone transfers changed, re-running cheap idempotent.

title: Upload files to a bucket — bucketUpload
code: bucketUpload(   local,   remotePath,   container = NULL,   remote = NULL,   filterFile = NULL,   transfers = 16L,   extra = character(0),   rclonePath = "rclone",   dryRun = FALSE,   echo = FALSE )

title: Upload files to a bucket — bucketUpload
text: local Path local file directory upload. remotePath Destination within bucket. Either key/prefix like "SCANFI_v2/1985", full public URL like "https://endpoint/container/SCANFI_v2/file.csv" — URL normalised key ("SCANFI_v2/file.csv") file lands expect rather nested bucket. Leading slashes required. container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. remote name local rclone remote. NULL, resolved options(buckethost.remote=), BUCKETHOST_REMOTE, built-default. filterFile Optional path rclone filter file (--filter-) include/exclude pattern. See migration vignette example. transfers Number parallel transfers (--transfers). Default 16. extra Character vector additional raw rclone flags, e.g. c("--retries", "10", "--stats", "60s"). rclonePath Path rclone executable. Default "rclone". dryRun TRUE, pass --dry-run rclone reports without transferring. Default FALSE. echo TRUE, print rclone command running .

title: Upload files to a bucket — bucketUpload
text: Invisibly, rclone's captured output (character).

title: Upload files to a bucket — bucketUpload
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bucketUpload("~/local/SCANFI/1985", "SCANFI_v2/1985",               filterFile = "~/scanfi.filter",               extra = c("--retries", "10", "--stats", "60s")) } # }

what: Build the public URL for an object — bucketUrl
title: Build the public URL for an object — bucketUrl
text: Build public URL object

title: Build the public URL for an object — bucketUrl
code: bucketUrl(path, container = NULL, endpoint = NULL)

title: Build the public URL for an object — bucketUrl
text: path Object key relative bucket root, e.g. "SCANFI_v2/1985/SCANFI_age_median_1985_v2.tif". container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. endpoint object store's HTTPS host (bucket name). NULL, resolved options(buckethost.endpoint=), BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, built-default.

title: Build the public URL for an object — bucketUrl
text: full HTTPS URL endpoint/container/path (character).

title: Build the public URL for an object — bucketUrl
code: bucketUrl("SCANFI_v2/1985/age.tif",            endpoint = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca",            container = "predictiveecology") #> [1] "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca/predictiveecology/SCANFI_v2/1985/age.tif"

what: Verify a local tree against the bucket — bucketVerify
title: Verify a local tree against the bucket — bucketVerify
text: Wraps rclone check compare local directory remote prefix upload. method = "size" (default, --size-) fast usually sufficient; method = "hash" compares checksums stronger, slower guarantee.

title: Verify a local tree against the bucket — bucketVerify
code: bucketVerify(   localDir,   remotePath,   container = NULL,   remote = NULL,   method = c("size", "hash"),   filterFile = NULL,   rclonePath = "rclone" )

title: Verify a local tree against the bucket — bucketVerify
text: localDir Local directory compare. remotePath Remote prefix within bucket compare . container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. remote name local rclone remote. NULL, resolved options(buckethost.remote=), BUCKETHOST_REMOTE, built-default. method "size" size-check (fast) "hash" checksum comparison (thorough). filterFile Optional rclone filter file, matching one used upload, comparison considers set files. rclonePath Path rclone executable. Default "rclone".

title: Verify a local tree against the bucket — bucketVerify
text: Invisibly, list ok (logical; TRUE rclone reports differences) output (rclone's captured stderr/stdout, lists mismatches).

title: Verify a local tree against the bucket — bucketVerify
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- bucketVerify("~/local/SCANFI/1985", "SCANFI_v2/1985",                      filterFile = "~/scanfi.filter") res$ok } # }

what: buckethost: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage — buckethost-package
title: buckethost: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage — buckethost-package
text: buckethost turns S3-compatible bucket browsable, remotely readable data repository. three jobs:

title: buckethost: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage — buckethost-package
text: Discover bucketLs() lists objects via bucket's public S3 listing API; bucketUrl() bucketRaster() build HTTPS / /vsicurl/ URLs read . Mutate bucketUpload() bucketDelete() wrap rclone transfers robust failures loud. Maintain generateIndexes() writes static index.html every "folder" bucket can browsed web browser; bucketVerify() checks local tree remote.

title: buckethost: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage — buckethost-package
text: functions default connection details options (falling back environment variables), set per session omit everywhere : equivalent environment variables BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, BUCKETHOST_REMOTE. See bucketConfig.
code: options(   buckethost.endpoint  = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca",   buckethost.container = "predictiveecology",   buckethost.remote    = "arbutus"   # the name of your rclone remote )

title: buckethost: Host and Catalogue Files on S3-Compatible Object Storage — buckethost-package
text: Maintainer: Eliot McIntire eliotmcintire@gmail.com Authors: Eliot McIntire eliotmcintire@gmail.com contributors: PredictiveEcology [copyright holder, funder]

what: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
text: Lists every object bucket, writes one index.html per "directory" level (including root) uploads back bucket rclone. result static, dependency-free website: open <base_url>/index.html browser navigate tree, sort columns, click files. page also carries configurable disclaimer/"hero" block context follows users wherever land.

title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
code: generateIndexes(   container = NULL,   endpoint = NULL,   remote = NULL,   heading = NULL,   disclaimerHtml = getOption("buckethost.disclaimerHtml", ""),   hostNote = getOption("buckethost.hostNote", "Hosted on an S3-compatible object store."),   template = NULL,   allFiles = NULL,   rclonePath = "rclone",   dryRun = FALSE,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
text: container bucket (container) name. NULL (default), resolved options(buckethost.container=), BUCKETHOST_CONTAINER, built-default. endpoint object store's HTTPS host (bucket name). NULL, resolved options(buckethost.endpoint=), BUCKETHOST_ENDPOINT, built-default. remote name local rclone remote. NULL, resolved options(buckethost.remote=), BUCKETHOST_REMOTE, built-default. heading Text page <h1> <title>. Defaults container name. Set repository's display name. disclaimerHtml Raw HTML inserted near top every page (yellow "hero" block default template). Use provenance notes, citations, links authoritative sources. Default "" (none). Can also set via options(buckethost.disclaimerHtml = ...). hostNote One line shown footer describing data hosted. Defaults via options(buckethost.hostNote = ...) generic string. template Path custom HTML template. Defaults one shipped package (see system.file("templates", "index.html", package = "buckethost")). Templates use {{token}} placeholders; see Details. allFiles Optional pre-fetched object table (returned bucketLs()). Supply avoid re-listing bucket, index subset. NULL (default) bucket listed . rclonePath Path rclone executable. Default "rclone". dryRun TRUE, build pages upload ; HTML returned (invisibly) inspection. Default FALSE. quiet Suppress progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
text: Invisibly, named character vector generated HTML pages (names directory paths, "" root).

title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
text: Re-run whenever bucket's contents change.

title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
text: custom template single HTML file tokens substituted per page: {{pageTitle}}, {{repoHeading}}, {{disclaimerHtml}}, {{viewingHeading}}, {{breadcrumb}}, {{parentLink}}, {{rows}}, {{hostNote}}, {{timestamp}}. shipped template provides sortable columns (vanilla JS, dependencies) directories pinned top.

title: Generate a browsable HTML catalogue for a bucket — generateIndexes
code:
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ options( buckethost.endpoint = "https://object-arbutus.cloud.computecanada.ca", buckethost.container = "predictiveecology", buckethost.remote = "arbutus" ) generateIndexes( heading = "PredictiveEcology Temporary Data Repository", disclaimerHtml = paste0( "

These data are not produced by the ", "PredictiveEcology group and are only hosted here to ease ", "open data access.

" ), hostNote = paste0("Hosted on the Digital Research Alliance of Canada's ", "Arbutus object storage.") ) } # }

what: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
title: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
text: Produces "remap" table: one row per object bucket, giving filename, full object key, public url. Optionally adds id column matching Google Drive file id, can map Drive source mirrored copy bucket. Optionally writes table CSV.

title: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
code: makeMirrorManifest(   bucket = NULL,   prefix = "",   driveFolder = NULL,   endpoint = NULL,   file = NULL )

title: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
text: bucket Container (bucket) name. NULL (default), resolved via bucketContainer(). prefix Optional key prefix restrict manifest, e.g. "SCANFI_v2/1985". Default "" (whole bucket). driveFolder Optional Google Drive folder id URL. supplied, folder listed recursively id column added, matched bucket objects file name. Requires googledrive package. endpoint Optional endpoint override (see bucketConfig). file Optional path write manifest CSV. NULL (default) file written.

title: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
text: data.frame columns filename, key, url, (driveFolder given) id. Returned invisibly file written, visibly otherwise.

title: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
text: Matching Drive file name (basename(key)). file name appears one folder bucket, rows told apart name alone id match ambiguous; warning issued listing offending names. key column always disambiguates rows .

title: Build a mirror manifest for a bucket — makeMirrorManifest
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ m <- buckethost::makeMirrorManifest(   "predictiveecology", "SCANFI_v2/",   driveFolder = "https://.../folders/199oEp-..." ) write.csv(m, "arbutus_manifest_SCANFI_v2_clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Or let makeMirrorManifest() write the CSV for you via `file =`: makeMirrorManifest("predictiveecology", "SCANFI_v2/",                    driveFolder = "https://.../folders/199oEp-...",                    file = "arbutus_manifest_SCANFI_v2_clean.csv") } # }

title: buckethost 0.1.0
text: functions multi-word arguments now camelCase, match PredictiveEcology package family. Renames: generate_indexes() -> generateIndexes(), bucket_ls() -> bucketLs(), bucket_url() -> bucketUrl(), bucket_raster() -> bucketRaster(), bucket_upload() -> bucketUpload(), bucket_delete() -> bucketDelete(), bucket_verify() -> bucketVerify(), bucket_endpoint() -> bucketEndpoint(), bucket_container() -> bucketContainer(), bucket_remote() -> bucketRemote(), bucket_base_url() -> bucketBaseUrl(), bucket_rclone_remote() -> bucketRcloneRemote(). Arguments likewise (e.g. dry_run -> dryRun, rclone_path -> rclonePath, filter_file -> filterFile, remote_path -> remotePath, disclaimer_html -> disclaimerHtml, host_note -> hostNote, all_files -> allFiles). Options follow: buckethost.disclaimerHtml, buckethost.hostNote.

title: buckethost 0.1.0
text: bucketUpload(), bucketDelete(), bucketVerify() now accept full public URL remotePath normalise object key. Previously passing e.g. "https://endpoint/container/SCANFI_v2/x.csv" uploaded file bogus nested key (URL embedded bucket) instead "SCANFI_v2/x.csv".

title: buckethost 0.0.3
text: New makeMirrorManifest(): builds “remap” data.frame (one row per object: filename, key, url), optionally adds Google Drive id column matched file name, optionally writes table CSV. Warns duplicate file names make Drive match ambiguous. googledrive moves Suggests.

title: buckethost 0.0.2
text: Fix generate_indexes() erroring “subscript bounds” actually uploading (dry_run): upload loop indexed pages name, root directory’s name "" pages[[""]] error R. now indexes positionally. Added regression test exercises full upload loop, including root page, rclone stubbed . generate_indexes() progress messages now report correct per-folder dir/file counts (previously lost vapply() stripped count attributes).

title: buckethost 0.0.1
text: Initial release. generate_indexes() writes browsable, sortable static HTML catalogue (one index.html per folder level) S3-compatible bucket uploads via rclone. HTML template externalised inst/templates/index.html {{token}} placeholders, page heading, disclaimer/“hero” block, footer note configurable. Discovery helpers: bucket_ls(), bucket_url(), bucket_raster(). Mutation helpers (rclone wrappers fail loudly): bucket_upload(), bucket_delete(). Maintenance: bucket_verify(). Connection configuration via options / BUCKETHOST_* environment variables: bucket_endpoint(), bucket_container(), bucket_remote(), bucket_base_url(), bucket_rclone_remote(). Vignettes: “Getting started” “Migrating data object store (Arbutus recipe)”.
